name: frontend
context: Frontend web development: React, CSS/Tailwind, state management, accessibility, bundling, and component testing
info: The frontend ecosystem covers modern web application development with React as the component framework, CSS/Tailwind for styling, state management with Zustand/Jotai/TanStack Query, accessibility-first design patterns, production bundling with Vite/esbuild, and component testing with Vitest and Playwright. This tree is organized around the jobs an agent actually performs: building components, styling interfaces, managing application state, ensuring accessibility compliance, bundling for production, and testing UI behavior.
body:
# Frontend Ecosystem

The frontend ecosystem covers building modern web applications with React, styling with CSS and Tailwind, managing state, ensuring accessibility, bundling for production, and testing components. The stack is TypeScript-first and targets both server-rendered and client-side applications.

## Agent Jobs

The primary tasks an agent performs in this ecosystem:

- **Build components** — create React components with hooks, context, and server components. See [[frontend/react]].
- **Style interfaces** — apply CSS, Tailwind utility classes, CSS Modules, or CSS-in-JS. See [[frontend/css]].
- **Manage state** — choose and implement the right state management pattern for the use case. See [[frontend/state]].
- **Ensure accessibility** — add ARIA roles, semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, and WCAG compliance. See [[frontend/accessibility]].
- **Bundle for production** — configure Vite or esbuild, tree-shake, code-split, and optimize output. See [[frontend/bundling]].
- **Test components** — write unit tests, component tests, and end-to-end tests. See [[frontend/testing]].

## Modern Stack

The recommended modern frontend stack:

| Layer | Tool | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| Framework | React 19 | Component model, server components |
| Language | TypeScript | Type safety, editor support |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS v4 | Utility-first CSS framework |
| State | Zustand / TanStack Query | Client state / server state |
| Bundler | Vite | Fast dev server, optimized builds |
| Testing | Vitest + Playwright | Unit/component + E2E |
| Hosting | Vercel / Cloudflare | Edge deployment, SSR |

## Build Tools

- **Vite** — lightning-fast dev server with HMR, Rollup-based production builds
- **esbuild** — extremely fast bundler/minifier written in Go
- **Turbopack** — Rust-based bundler for Next.js (incremental computation)
- **Biome** — fast formatter and linter replacing ESLint + Prettier
- **TypeScript** — static type checking, interfaces, generics

sections.title: Ecosystem overview
sections.body: The frontend ecosystem is organized around agent jobs: building React components ([[frontend/react]]), styling with CSS/Tailwind ([[frontend/css]]), managing state ([[frontend/state]]), ensuring accessibility ([[frontend/accessibility]]), bundling for production ([[frontend/bundling]]), and testing ([[frontend/testing]]). TypeScript is the default language across all skills.

sections.title: Modern frontend stack
sections.body: React 19 with server components, TypeScript for type safety, Tailwind CSS v4 for utility-first styling, Zustand for client state, TanStack Query for server state, Vite for bundling, Vitest and Playwright for testing. Deploy to Vercel or Cloudflare for edge rendering and SSR.

sections.title: Build tools
sections.body: Vite provides fast HMR in development and optimized Rollup-based production builds. esbuild handles transpilation and minification at Go speed. Turbopack is the Rust-based Next.js bundler. Biome replaces ESLint + Prettier with a single fast tool. See [[frontend/bundling]] for configuration details.
